paper_title: Analysis of Planktonic Foraminifera in Sulawesi Sea

methods.method_name: Sediment Sample Preparation and Foraminifera Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing sediment samples and analyzing foraminifera to determine their relative abundance and ecological or functional types.

steps.description: Ten surface sediment samples (S-1 to S-10) from sediment core in Sulawesi Sea were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Sulawesi Sea

steps.description: The sediment is soaked in water to separate the foraminifera test from sediment grains, and then samples were washed while filtered by using a 150 µm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: separate foraminifera from sediment using water and sieve

steps.description: Sediment samples larger than 150 µm size were dried in the oven with 40 O -60 O C.
About 300 specimens of foraminifera were separated from other materials for quantitative analysis.
Foraminifera which has been separated from other materials was then identified using a binocular microscope following several identification references.
Relative abundance is the percentage of each species in each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundance of foraminifera species

methods.description: This method involves the use of the Cluster Method to perform biozonation analysis, identifying distinct biozones based on environmental conditions and species composition.

steps.description: Biozonation analysis is based on calculations using the Cluster Method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform biozonation analysis using Cluster Method

steps.description: Two biozones from the Cluster Method are obtained and described by a dendrogram.
